commit.message:
Fix 10 security vulnerabilities

- Bound stdin/file input to 100MB to prevent memory exhaustion DoS
- Cap wildcard extraction results at 1M to prevent cartesian explosion
- Replace deprecated serde_yaml with maintained serde_yml 0.0.12
- Process escape sequences in .env double-quoted values
- Combine duplicate HTTP headers per RFC 9110 instead of silent overwrite
- Error on unterminated quoted values in logfmt parser
- Support escape sequences in quoted selector keys
- Use i64::try_from for safe array length cast in negative indexing
- Replace sed with node for safe JSON manipulation in build script

commit.message:
Fix continuous background playback for bilingual mode with mixed TTS engines (#90)

* Fix continuous background playback for bilingual mode with mixed TTS engines

Ensure native iOS TTS (AVSpeechSynthesizer) properly maintains audio session
activation when used in continuous playback mode alongside VOICEVOX. This fix
applies the same audio session management pattern to native TTS that was
previously applied only to VOICEVOX playback.

**Issue:**
When bilingual mode plays both English (native TTS) and Japanese (VOICEVOX)
consecutively in background mode, the audio session context was lost between
transitions, causing continuous playback to stop after the first sentence.

**Root Cause:**
Native TTS relied on a simple activateAudioSession() helper that only called
setActive(true), while VOICEVOX explicitly set both category and activation
right before playback. In background mode with mixed engines, the session
needed explicit category reconfiguration for each playback.

**Solution:**
Apply consistent audio session management for both TTS engines:
- Set category to .playback mode right before playback
- Activate session in the same execution context as speech start
- Mirrors the pattern already working for VOICEVOX (lines 181-183)

This ensures iOS recognizes each TTS call as background-capable, maintaining
seamless continuous playback when switching between engines.

**Testing:**
- Build and run on physical iOS device
- Configure bilingual playback mode with native English TTS and VOICEVOX Japanese
- Start continuous playback and send app to background
- Verify playback continues through all sentences without stopping

Closes #89

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix continuous playback by avoiding category reconfiguration during playback

When continuous playback is active, avoid reconfiguring the audio session
category if it's already set to .playback. This prevents interruption of
the audio session that was causing playback to stop after one sentence.

**Changes:**
- Check isContinuousPlayback flag and current category before calling setCategory()
- Only reconfigure category when starting fresh or if category has changed
- Always activate the audio session to ensure background playback

**Technical details:**
The previous fix unconditionally called setCategory() for every speak() call,
which caused the audio session to reset during continuous playback transitions.
This new approach preserves the audio session state during continuous playback
while still ensuring it's properly configured for background mode.

**Testing:**
- Verify EN→JA→EN playback continues to next sentence in background mode
- Test bilingual mode with mixed TTS engines (native + VOICEVOX)
- Confirm individual play buttons still work correctly

Related to #89

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: correct claude CLI invocation, dashboard stats, and Next.js fetch caching

Worker: `claude run <skill>` doesn't exist. Now reads skill rawContent from DB,
strips front-matter (gray-matter), injects inputs as JSON block, and invokes:
  claude -p "<instructions + inputs>" --dangerously-skip-permissions

Dashboard: stats now come from GET /api/stats (Prisma groupBy by status) instead
of counting statuses from a paginated 10-task slice. Fixes 0-count bug.

api.ts: added cache: 'no-store' to all fetches. force-dynamic alone doesn't
bypass Next.js App Router fetch cache — stale data was showing on navigation.

Also updates learnings.md (3 new lessons) and PLAN.md checkboxes.

New products:

- CardCraft: testimonial card generator ($9.99)

- PolicyForge: privacy policy generator ($12.99)

- 5 industry-specific SEO landing pages on PolicyForge
- JSON-LD structured data for rich search results

- Added all 4 new products to projects list

commit.message:
feat(e2e): add GUI bootstrap E2E test and harden build scripts with rch worker probing

test_ft_1memj_26_gui_bootstrap.sh (387 lines, new):
- Comprehensive E2E test for the GUI bootstrap pipeline covering:
  rch worker probe failure handling, bundle creation with overwrite
  refusal, e2e_gui_bootstrap.sh dry-run and skip-build modes,
  and artifact directory structure validation
- Each scenario emits structured JSONL logs with correlation IDs,
  decision paths, and artifact paths for post-mortem analysis
- Uses mock rch/codesign/cargo binaries to test failure paths
  without requiring actual remote workers or Xcode tooling

create-macos-bundle.sh:
- Add rch worker probing before attempting remote cargo build —
  refuses to proceed if no reachable workers are detected, preventing
  silent local cargo fallback that would produce wrong-arch binaries
- Replace destructive rm -rf of existing .app bundle with a safety
  check that refuses to overwrite, requiring manual removal or a
  fresh --output directory
- Add RCH_BIN environment variable for configurable rch path

e2e_gui_bootstrap.sh:
- Add probe_rch_workers() function that calls rch workers probe,
  saves probe JSON to a log file, and filters out failed/unreachable
  workers before deciding whether to proceed
- Use unique bundle output directories (bundle-${BUILD_PROFILE}-$$)
  to avoid collisions between concurrent or repeated test runs
- Add RCH_PROBE_LOG variable for probe result persistence
- Wire probe check into build_gui() with early exit on failure
- Add dry-run guard for build step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: org admin DEA access, audit trail, and backend hardening (#106)

* feat(auth): allow org admins/editors to access admin DEA routes

Replace requireAdmin() with requireAdminOrAedPermission() in admin DEA
API routes so organization members with can_edit or can_view permissions
can access /api/admin/deas/[id] without needing global ADMIN role.

- Add requireAdminOrAedPermission helper that checks global ADMIN role
  first (zero extra DB queries), then falls back to org-level permission
  check via getUserPermissionsForAed (2 DB queries)
- GET /api/admin/deas/[id]: requires can_view (editors + verifiers)
- PATCH /api/admin/deas/[id]: requires can_edit (editors only)
- POST /api/admin/deas/[id]/process-image: requires can_edit
- Update org DEA list page to use adminMode for users with canEdit or
  canVerify permissions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(verify): redesign verification filters based on actual need

Replace publication-status-based filters with verification-need-based
filters that better reflect the actual workflow:

- "never_verified": DEAs without any manual verification (was "pending")
- "requires_attention": DEAs flagged for review (requires_attention=true)
- "verification_expired": DEAs with verification older than 6 months

Also remove the is_verified user check from /verify/[id] that was
redirecting org verifiers to the home page — org-level can_verify
permission is sufficient.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: comprehensive lifecycle workflows v2 with code audit findings

Complete rewrite based on thorough audit of all source code covering
admin routes, verification flow, organization assignments, audit
trails, publication system, and public API routes. Documents 12
critical bugs/gaps found and prioritized implementation roadmap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(lib): add AED status state machine and audit trail helpers

Centralize audit trail logic (previously duplicated in 4-5 routes) into
shared helpers. Add status transition validation state machine for
enforcing valid AED lifecycle transitions.

- aed-status.ts: state machine with VALID_TRANSITIONS map, validates
  DRAFT→PENDING_REVIEW→PUBLISHED→INACTIVE flows
- audit.ts: recordStatusChange(), recordFieldChange(), appendInternalNote()
  helpers that accept both prisma client and transaction client

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(verify): wrap DB writes in transactions and use audit helpers

- verify/duplicates: remove dead if(!user) checks, wrap both actions
  (not_duplicate, confirm_duplicate) in $transaction, use
  appendInternalNote() and recordFieldChange()/recordStatusChange()
  helpers, add validateStatusTransition() for REJECTED transition
- verify/complete: restructure into 2 phases — S3 uploads outside
  transaction, all DB writes (image updates, validation completion,
  AED status update, status audit, org verification) inside single
  $transaction. Add validateStatusTransition() for PUBLISHED transition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(admin): remove dead code, use audit helpers, add transactions

Remove 15+ unreachable if(!user/admin) null checks across 7 admin
routes — requireAuth/requireAdmin throw AuthError, never return null.

- admin/deas/[id]: extract TRACKABLE_AED_FIELDS/ALLOWED_AED_FIELDS
  constants, use recordStatusChange() helper in transaction
- admin/organizations verifications POST: wrap in $transaction
- admin/organizations routes: remove dead checks in GET/PATCH/DELETE
- admin/members routes: remove dead checks, keep admin var where used
- admin/assignments routes: remove dead checks
- aeds/[id]/publication: remove dead 10-line unreachable block
- organization-permissions: add checkAssignmentConflict validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(stats): remove double-counting of pending DEAs in org stats

pendingVerifications (DEAs needing re-verification) was being added to
deasByStatusMap.pending (DEAs in DRAFT/PENDING_REVIEW), causing DEAs
that are both DRAFT and unverified to be counted twice in the total.

Also removes dead if(!user) null check after requireAuth().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(process-image): restructure into 4 phases with transaction

Reorganize image processing endpoint into clear phases:
1. Resolve original image (validation + download — network I/O)
2. Process with Sharp (CPU work)
3. Upload to S3 (network I/O)
4. All DB writes in single $transaction (image update, audit trail
   via recordFieldChange(), AED timestamp update)

Uses requireAdminOrAedPermission for org-level can_edit access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md with new backend conventions

Add documentation for:
- Auth helpers throw pattern (never add null checks after requireAuth)
- Transaction requirement for multi-write operations
- Audit trail helpers (recordStatusChange, recordFieldChange, appendInternalNote)
- AED status state machine (validateStatusTransition)
- Organization permissions (requireAdminOrAedPermission, getUserPermissionsForAed)
- Field allowlist constants pattern
- Updated directory structure with new lib/ files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): rejected DEAs reappearing in verification list

- Add status filter to verification list query (exclude DRAFT/REJECTED/INACTIVE)
- Add PUBLISHED → REJECTED transition to state machine (verifiers can reject published DEAs)
- Add validateStatusTransition() to PATCH /api/aeds/[id] to prevent invalid transitions
- Capture rejection_reason in status change history record

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): remove redundant ...validationData spread in step transitions

The crop→blur and blur→arrow transitions were spreading ALL accumulated
validation data into each PUT payload, causing:
- Bloated payloads with stale fields (e.g., current_step: "image_blur"
  when step is "image_arrow")
- Premature data appearing (responsible_data before responsible step)

The server PUT handler already merges new step data with existing
validation.data, so only the new data for each step needs to be sent.
The client-side optimistic update also does the same merge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): persist processed image previews for REVIEW step reload

Include processed_url (data: URL from ArrowPlacer) in the
ProcessedImageData sent to the server. Previously it was kept
client-side only, which meant reloading the page on the REVIEW
step showed original images instead of the crop+blur+arrow previews.

The REVIEW step now falls back to the server-persisted processed_url
when the client-side localImageUrls is empty (e.g., after reload).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): model file 404s and 413 payload too large

- Rename face-api model shard files to match manifest paths (.bin)
  so loadFromUri resolves them locally instead of falling back to CDN
- Strip processed_url (base64 data URLs) from processed_images before
  sending PUT to server, keeping payload under Vercel's body-size limit
- Client-side previews still work during the session; after reload the
  REVIEW step shows original images with processing badges

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(verify): regenerate processed image previews on REVIEW reload

After stripping base64 data URLs from the server payload (413 fix),
processed images were lost on page reload. Now the REVIEW step
regenerates them client-side from stored parameters (crop_data,
blur_areas, arrow_data) using an off-screen canvas pipeline.

- Add src/utils/imageRegeneration.ts: pure canvas utility that
  applies crop → blur → arrow in sequence, reusing loadImageWithRetry
  and ARROW_CONFIG from existing utilities
- Add useEffect in verify page that triggers regeneration when
  entering REVIEW step with missing local preview URLs
- Show spinner while regenerating

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): allow verification to publish AEDs from any state

Completing a verification IS the approval mechanism — the user went
through the full flow (address, images, responsible, review). Remove
the state machine check from the complete endpoint so it always sets
status to PUBLISHED regardless of previous state.

- Remove validateStatusTransition check from complete/route.ts
- Add status guard in GET /verify/[id]: non-admins cannot verify
  AEDs in REJECTED/DRAFT/INACTIVE states; admins see a warning
- Show status_warning banner in frontend for non-standard states

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(verify): add "Descartados" filter for rejected AEDs (admin-only)

Admins can now filter the verification list by rejected/discarded AEDs
to re-verify them. The filter is only visible and accessible to admins.

- Add "rejected" to VerificationFilterType
- Add case in getVerifiableAedsForUser switch (status: "REJECTED")
- Add admin-only guard in API route (403 for non-admins)
- Show "Descartados" tab in frontend only when isAdmin is true

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(stats): align pending verifications count with verification list filter

The org stats endpoint counted AEDs pending verification without filtering
by status, including REJECTED/DRAFT/INACTIVE AEDs. The verification list
only shows PUBLISHED/PENDING_REVIEW AEDs, causing a mismatch where the
org page showed "15 pendientes" but the list was empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(admin): add individual and batch AED deletion (admin-only)

- Add DELETE method to /api/admin/deas/[id] for individual AED deletion
  with orphan cleanup (location, schedule) and required reason
- Add DELETE /api/admin/deas/batch for batch deletion (max 50, dry-run support)
- Add red "Eliminar" button in admin DEA detail page with ConfirmDialog
- Add batch selection mode in verify page (Descartados filter only):
  checkboxes, select all, floating action bar
- Adapt cleanup endpoint to also target REJECTED AEDs (not just INACTIVE)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): allow DRAFT AEDs to be verified by any user

DRAFT is a valid verifiable state — verification is the mechanism to
review and publish a DEA. The status guard was incorrectly blocking
non-admin users from verifying DRAFT AEDs. Updated VERIFIABLE_STATUSES
to include DRAFT across verify route, org-permissions filter, and stats.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): clamp crop/blur coordinates to image bounds

Sharp throws "extract_area: bad extract area" when crop or blur
coordinates exceed the actual image dimensions. This happens because
the frontend stores coordinates from a canvas that may differ from
the server-side image size. Clamp all extract coordinates to valid
bounds before processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(verify): make image processing/upload errors non-fatal during completion

Individual image processing or S3 upload failures no longer abort the
entire verification. Errors are collected per-image and the verification
completes with warnings. The response includes a `warnings` array so the
client can display partial-failure info to the user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: centralize AED status labels, colors and filter options

Create `src/lib/aed-status-config.ts` as single source of truth for
status labels (singular/plural), badge colors, and role-based filter
options (`visibleToAll` flag gates REJECTED/INACTIVE to admins only).

Fix org DEAs page: filter by AED status (`aed_status`) instead of
assignment status (`status: active/inactive`) which always showed
"Inactivos" as empty.

Add AED status badge to DeaListItem component. Replace 6 hardcoded
label/color definitions across the codebase with centralized imports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): ignore aed_status=all instead of passing it to Prisma

The filter dropdown sends "all" as a value when no specific status is
selected. The API must treat it as "no filter" instead of querying for
a non-existent "all" status enum value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show all AED statuses to org members and make dashboard stats clickable

Organizations are autonomous — they need to see all DEA statuses
(including INACTIVE and REJECTED) without depending on global admins.

- Make INACTIVE/REJECTED visible to all users in filter dropdowns
- Stats API now returns per-status counts instead of grouped buckets
- Dashboard "Estado de los DEAs" shows individual statuses from config
- Each status row links to /org/.../deas?aed_status=X with pre-applied filter
- DeasList reads URL search params to initialize filters on navigation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): wrap useSearchParams in Suspense boundary

Next.js 15+ requires useSearchParams to be inside a Suspense boundary
for static page generation. Wrap DeasList internally so all consumers
get the boundary automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: allow org editors to delete DEAs assigned to their organization

Change DELETE /api/admin/deas/[id] from requireAdmin (global admin only)
to requireAdminOrAedPermission with can_edit flag, consistent with
GET and PATCH. Organizations should be autonomous and not depend on
global admins to manage their DEAs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test+fix: add 59 unit tests and fix review issues

Tests:
- 27 tests for aed-status-config (labels, colors, filters, visibility rules)
- 32 tests for aed-status (state machine transitions, valid/invalid paths)

Fixes from code review:
- Remove dead code: if (!user) after requireAuth in /api/deas (CLAUDE.md rule)
- DRY: import AedStatus type from aed-status-config instead of duplicating
- Performance: parallelize 3 sequential count() queries with Promise.all
- Fix trailing ? in batch delete URL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: fix review issues — S3 outside tx, proper types, no prop mutation

- Move S3 uploadToS3 calls outside prisma.$transaction (only DB writes inside)
- Replace Record<string, any> with AedWhereInput/AedOrganizationAssignmentWhereInput
- Remove prop mutation (orgFilter.options = ...) — use useMemo-derived resolvedFilters
- Type paginationInfo as PaginationInfo | null instead of any
- Fix adminMode: only canEdit users get admin links (verifiers see public view)
- Replace catch(err: any) with catch(err: unknown) + instanceof check
- Type org map callback properly instead of any

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add unit tests for TasteEngine, DietaryFilterHelper, and DateUtils

Extracted testable logic from activities into a utils/ package:
- DateUtils.getCurrentWeekStart() — moved from RecipeDetailActivity
- DietaryFilterHelper.deriveDietaryFilters() — moved from VivianActivity
Both activity methods now delegate to the utils; call sites unchanged.

31 pure JUnit tests (no Android context required):
- TasteEngineTest: getTopCuisine (null/empty/single/multi), getSimilarCuisines
  (known, unknown, no-self, returns new list), getAdventurousCuisines
  (no origin, no direct neighbors, two-hop content, unknown, no duplicates)
- DietaryFilterHelperTest: null/empty/unsupported prefs, diet precedence
  (vegan > vegetarian), individual and combined intolerances, all-prefs case
- DateUtilsTest: returns Monday, returns midnight, value is seconds not ms,
  not in future, within last 7 days

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix TUI wrapping: wrap plain text before styling to avoid ANSI interference

The previous approach styled text first then applied lipgloss Width(),
which could conflict with the viewport's own Width() render pass.
Now wraps plain text with wordwrap.String before applying ANSI styles,
and accounts for label width on the first line.

Added width diagnostic [w:N] to status bar for debugging.
Added wrapping coverage to content_test.go.
